course_title: Books the Nazis Burned: German Literature & Society 1914-1945

learning_outcomes.description: analyse some significant texts of the period indicated in their historical and social contexts
integrate significant aspects of the liberal-democratic tradition in German culture in the period indicated into your textual analysis
articulate and defend your conclusions in seminar discussions and in formal academic writing

assessments.task_description:
This exam will be held in the first class in Week 6. Students will be required to respond to questions from a wider selection. The questions will refer to themes and texts discussed in the first five weeks of class.

The duration of the exam is 90 minutes (no reading time).

AI/MT Statement

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date:
6/10/2026 - 9/10/2026

Individual interview will be scheduled no later than 5pm Friday, WEEK 10.

Interviews will be conducted outside of class time.
assessments.task_description:
The interview will focus on a text studied in class during Weeks 7–9.

The interview will last up to 15 minutes. The goal of this assessment task is to give students the opportunity to focus on a single text and relate their reading to the themes of the course.

Guidelines for the interview will be circulated in Week 9.

AI/MT Statement

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
An end-of-semester examination of 120 minutes.

Students will be asked to write responses to two questions from a wider selection.

The aim of the assessment is to allow students the opportunity to reflect on the themes of the course. Students will be required to consult the recommended readings for the course as part of this relection.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Appropriate Preparation

Throughout the semester, students are expected to prepare constructively for lectures by reading the relevant required texts, and by noting the value of re-reading.
